title: Tympanic Temperature Measurement
content_markdown:
#### Indications
- As part of a normal patient assessment.

#### Procedure

- Place a clean probe over the thermometer, this will activate the thermometer.

- Wait for the signalling “on” beep and then insert probe snugly into the ear canal.

- Push and release the start button.

- Wait for the “completion” beep and then withdraw the probe from the ear.

- If the procedure has been completed correctly, the temperature measurement will be displayed on the screen.

- If the procedure has not been successful, a sequence of short beeps will sound and an error message will show. Attempt the procedure once more.

- Once a result has been recorded, press the probe ejector button and dispose of the used probe cover.

- The thermometer will automatically turn off after 60 seconds.

#### Contraindications
- Obvious trauma or infection to the ear.

#### Specific Notes
- Ambient temperatures below 10\xb0C and above 40\xb0C may produce inaccurate results.
- A reading displayed as “HI” or “LOW” indicates temperatures 42.2\xb0C.
- Temperature readings should always be considered in relationship to patient presentation as there are a number of things that can result in inaccurate readings.
